title: After-Action Report — Substitution tags stripped by `_stat_delta`
scope: Three-layer bug chain in substitution rendering pipeline where identity tags (entry_type, replaced_player_id, entered_inning) were lost between renderer and database persistence, causing box scores to show flat substitution data

rationale.item: Post-merge box scores showed jokers mid-lineup, duplicate DEF subs, phantom starter rows, and zero footnote letters despite engine producing correct stamps
references: user bug report on game #53 Missions 39 Sea Dogs 25

actions.description: Fixed renderer key-mismatch: substitution handlers now read correct provider event keys (player_in/player_out/runner_in/joker) instead of manager-side keys
Added SQL-level fallback to recover joker entry_type from permanent is_joker identity flag for legacy games
Fixed _stat_delta to preserve identity tags when constructing per-phase delta rows, the actual root cause
Added regression test for entry_type tag preservation through _stat_delta

risk_posture: Multi-layer diagnostic required: three separate bugs, each masking the one below
Solution is minimal (three lines in _stat_delta) but finding required unwinding two earlier fixes
Old games with already-persisted bad data are unrecoverable except for jokers (via is_joker fallback)

validation_claims.claim: 30-game synthetic sim: 5 jokers, 2 DEF subs, 1 PH — all stamped properly with correct entry_type and entered_inning
validation_claims.check_method: Run 30-game sim with substitution instrumentation and inspect game_batter_stats rows

non_actions.item: Re-simulation of affected games from before fix
non_actions.reason: Recommended but not done. Once a sub event fires without stamping entry_type, the DB row has no signal to reconstruct it from (except jokers). Re-simulating affected games is the only way to recover sub indentation, ph/pr labels, and footnotes.

residual_risks: Old games: unrecoverable except for jokers via is_joker fallback
Process lesson: multi-layer pipelines require sampling field of interest at every layer between source and destination, even obvious pass-throughs
open_hooks: Consider re-simulating affected games from bug window if presentation fidelity on early-season box scores matters
Establish diagnostic protocol for multi-layer pipeline: test sim → renderer cumulative → renderer per-phase delta → DB row at each point
section_headers_original: Layer 1 — Renderer key-mismatch (shipped as fec36ab on main, also a185154 on this branch)
Layer 2 — Legacy joker rows had entry_type='starter' (shipped as 0cd078d)
Layer 3 — _stat_delta strips identity tags (shipped as 7952fec, the actual root cause)
Why two rounds of 'fixing' didn't catch the third bug
unmapped_content: Branch: claude/normalize-game-scores-3AXlu. PRs: #74 → #78 → #79 → #80. Final commit: 7952fec. Root cause: _stat_delta at render.py:1665 constructed per-phase delta rows by subtracting counters but didn't copy identity fields (entry_type, replaced_player_id, entered_inning) which are static flags set once and never incremented. Fix is three-line addition of identity field assignments at top of _stat_delta. Process lesson covers three rounds of diagnosis: Layer 1 diagnosed by instrumenting should_* call counts vs events in cumulative dict (showed 168 PH / 4 PR / 54 DEF landing correctly); Layer 2 caught by user reporting jokers still mid-lineup post-fix; Layer 3 caught when inspecting batter_stats_for_phase delta dict instead of cumulative dict. General pattern: sampling field of interest at single layer isn't enough in multi-layer transformations.
parser_notes: High-quality diagnostic AAR documenting three-layer bug chain with clear root-cause analysis and process lesson for future multi-layer pipeline debugging. No explicit delegation events but describes decision to add regression test and not touch legacy data.
